rulings.Case Title: FLORA NG VS. STERLING REAL ESTATE PARNERS II, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 4, 2010, line 2 DEFENDANT STERLING PACIFIC LENDING, INC.,DBA STERLING, YOSEMITE MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THOSE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT REGARDING NEW NOTICES. SUSTAINED WITHE 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE DEFECTIVE NOTICE. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED THAT THE AMOUNT STATED IN THE NOTICES WAS "SUBSTANTIALLY MORE" THAN THE AMOUNT OWED(PARAGRAPHS 27B,33B,39B). HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD A SUBSTANTIAL AND PREJUDICIAL DEFECTIVE NOTICE OF SOLE. KNAPP V. DOHERTY (2004) 123 CAL APP 4TH 76. THE COURT STRIKES THE LATE FILED SUR-REPLY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FLORA NG VS. STERLING REAL ESTATE PARNERS II, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltf'S First Amended Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 4, 2010, line 2 DEFENDANT STERLING PACIFIC LENDING, INC.,DBA STERLING, YOSEMITE MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC'S Notice And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THOSE PORTIONS REGARDING NEW NOTICES. IF THE LAW CHANGES, PLAINTIFF MAY BRING A MOTION TO AMNED. DENIED AS TO THOSE PORTIONS REGARDING ATTORNEY FEES. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT THE PROMISSORY NOTES CCONTAIN ATTORNEY FEE CLAUSES LEADING TO A FACTUAL DISPUTE NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DEMURRER. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NORTH POINT HOLDINGS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. PAUL F UTRECHT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 4, 2010, line 3 DEFENDANT PAUL UTRECHT, ZACKS & UTECHT, P.C. F/K/A ZACKS, UTRECHT & LEADBETTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD IS NOT HEARSAY AND IS PROPERLY REVIEWED BY THE COURT IN DETERMING WHETHER PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE PREVAILED IN THE UDERLYING ACTION. FURTHER BRIEFING IS REQUESTED ON TWO ISUUES: 1) WHETHER THE PETITION WAS PREMATURE UNDER PUBLIC RESOURCES CODE 21167; AND 2) WHETHER PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE PREVAILED IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS. HEARING CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 2. FURTHER OPPOSITION TO BE FILED AND PERSONALLY SERVED BY NOVEMBER 17. FURTHER REPLY FILED AND PERSONALLY SERVED BY NOVEMBER 29. COURTESY COPIES WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPT. 302. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAPHIRE ADVISORS LLC, VS. THE SAINT JAMES EOS WINE COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 4, 2010, line 10 DEFENDANT WAYNE GRONQUIST, GEORGE MCCARTHY, HURST, RICHARD'S Notice And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. THIS HEARING WAS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. TAYLOR-RUSH V MULTI TECH CORPORATION (1990) 217 CAL APP 3RD 103 IS ON POINT. THIS HEARING IS FURTHER CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 15, 2010 TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO CONDUCT LIMITED FURTHER DISCOVERY THROUGH DEPOSITIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS PARTICIPATED IN OR DIRECTED A TORTIOUS ACT OR OMMISSION EITHER WITHIN OR WITHOUT CALIFORNIA. SEE TAYLOR V. RUSH AT PAGE 114. FURTHER OPPOSITION DUE DECEMBER 2. FURTHER REPLY DUE DECEMBER 8. COURTESY COPIES DELIVERED TO DEPT. 302. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW S. PARK et al VS. R.C. CHRONICLE BUILDING L.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Andrew S. Park And Annie J. I. Kim'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 4, 2010, line 8 Plaintiffs Andrew S. Park And Annie J. I. Kim's Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S ARE UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS AND PLAINTIFFS' HARM IS NOT IRREPARABLE. PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENTIARY SHOWING IS WEAK AND PLAINTIFFS ADMIT PRESENTING UNTRUTHFUL LOAN DOCUMENTS. THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT THAT THE NOTE BE PRODUCED. PLAINTIFF'S NO LONGER LIVE AT THE PREMISES. DFENDANT HAS PRODUCED DOCUMENTATION SHOWING THAT HOUSEKEY HAS SUBSTITUTED IN AS THE TRUSTEE. PLAINTIFFS' MAY NOT RAISE A NEW ARGUMENT IN THEIR REPLY REGARDING THE BOOK NUMBER, AND IN SUFFICIENT AUTHORITY PRESENTED THAT SUCH AN ERROR WOULD RENDER THE SALE VOID AS OPPOSED TO VOIDABLE. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT TENDERED. THE LENDER IS NOT REQUIRED TO MODIFY THE LOAN UNDER THE CIVIL CODE, AND DFENDANT PRESENTS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF DUE DILLIGENCE. =(302/CWW)
